encounterLog.description: Rain has followed you the whole way from the coach road. The waystation is warm, low-ceilinged, and full of the smell of tallow and wet wool. Behind the long bar a broad man polishes a cup he has already polished twice; at a corner table a local nurses a mug and watches you over the rim. Your cape is still stiff with road-dust. You are the only Cape these people have seen in a long while, and they all know it before you have said a word. Somewhere in your satchel is a sealed writ: walk the Reach Circuit, recertify its wardstones — routine work — and "ascertain the standing" of Senior Cape Edran Vael, who has not filed a report in three seasons.

lines.text: [Halden Marrick says to Local Villager]: hello
💬 Local Villager: "Evening. You're a long way from a city, Cape."

The villager glances up from their mug, eyes sliding briefly to the white of your cape before settling on your face — a quick calculation happening behind cautious eyes.
They wrap both hands around their mug and don't quite smile — not unfriendly, but not open either. Across the room, the tavern keeper sets down his polishing cloth and watches without pretending not to.
